summary:
The adult spinal cord typically extends down to the lower border of the first lumbar vertebra (L1), with occasional variation up to the upper edge of L2 where it terminates as a cone-shaped structure known as the conus medullaris.
To refine further while keeping within two concise sentences:

The adult spinal cord usually reaches the lower margin of the first lumbar vertebra (L1). It may extend slightly higher, sometimes ending around the upper part of L2 before tapering into the conus medullaris. To ensure clarity without redundancy,
Sentence one focuses solely on location for simplicity, then sentence two adds critical detail concisely regarding variations observed clinically but omits 'conus medullaris' term which was already covered initially. In adults, the spinal cord generally extends to the lower border of L1; can vary rarely extending to upper L2.
